commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

commit.message:
test: write-path safety validation tests (#535)

Validates data integrity contracts: partial write detection, size mismatch
handling, delete safety, journal correctness, concurrent write serialization.

34 tests covering:
- sendObject size mismatch via postWriteVerify
- Interrupted transfer journal state tracking
- Journal state transition ordering
- Delete on read-only storage detection
- Delete with invalid/non-existent handles
- Zero-byte and empty-data write edge cases
- Concurrent writes serialized through actor
- Write after device disconnect (fault injection)
- Journal records write before transfer starts
- Journal completes only after explicit call
- Failed transfers excluded from resumables
- Retry classification for transport/protocol errors
- verificationFailed error semantics
- Write-read round-trip data integrity
- Remote handle tracking in journal

commit.message:
wave49: Canon EOS & Nikon PTP camera quirks research (#534)

Add comprehensive PTP vendor extension research for Canon EOS and Nikon
DSLR/mirrorless cameras based on libgphoto2 and libmtp sources.

Documentation (Docs/camera-ptp-research.md):
- Canon EOS vendor operations (0x9001-0x903F classic, 0x9101-0x91FF EOS)
- Nikon vendor operations (0x90C0-0x90CF, 0x9200-0x9207, 0x9400-0x9436)
- Canon/Nikon event codes, response codes, object format codes
- Complete Nikon Z-series PID table (Z7 through Z6 III)
- Canon EOS Rebel PID reference table
- Key quirks: DONT_CLOSE_SESSION, DeviceReady polling, vendor
  extension ID override, keep-alive requirements, broken MTP proplists
- Timeout recommendations from libgphoto2 constants
- Implementation roadmap (read-only → capture → wireless)

Quirks updates (Specs/quirks.json):
- Enhanced canon-eos-rebel-3139 notes with 17 research findings
  including DONT_CLOSE_SESSION for 2016+ EOS, DELETE_SENDS_EVENT,
  64-bit operations, Canon start timeout (1500ms), CR3 format codes
- Note that PID 0x3139 is actually PowerShot A640 (not EOS Rebel)
- Enhanced nikon-dslr-0410 notes with 17 research findings including
  DeviceReady busy-wait protocol, hidden vendor ops on D3xxx/D5xxx,
  Nikon 1 series broken GetEvent, Z-series PID table
- Both entries now reference primary research sources

commit.message:
Port codebase from Qt5 to Qt6 (#15)

* Initial plan

* Update codebase for Qt6 compatibility

- Replace QDesktopWidget with QScreen/QGuiApplication (workoutdialog.cpp)
- Replace QRegExp with QRegularExpression (util.cpp, importerworkout.cpp)
- Replace QStyleOption::init() with initFrom() (workoutcreator.cpp, main_workoutpage.cpp, main_coursepage.cpp, googlemapwidget.cpp)
- Replace QWebEngineSettings::globalSettings() with QWebEngineProfile::defaultProfile()->settings() (globalvars.cpp, webbrowserv2.cpp)
- Guard Qt::AA_EnableHighDpiScaling with Qt version check (globalvars.cpp)
- Remove QT += macextras (unused, removed in Qt6)
- Update CONFIG c++11 to c++17 (required by Qt6)
- Remove Qt4/Qt5 version conditionals in VLC-Qt widget files
- Remove QDesktopWidget include from WidgetVideo.cpp (unused)

Co-authored-by: MaximumTrainer <1376575+MaximumTrainer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Optimize QRegularExpression usage in importerworkout.cpp

Co-authored-by: MaximumTrainer <1376575+MaximumTrainer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix compilation errors for Qt5/Qt6 cross-platform builds

   Four targeted fixes to resolve build failures on Linux, macOS, and Windows:

   src/_subclassQWT/myqwtdial.cpp
   - Replace setPenWidthF(2.0) with setPenWidth(2): QwtRoundScaleDraw has
     no setPenWidthF member. Fixes hard compile error on Linux.

   src/_subclassQWT/workoutplotlist.cpp
   - Add missing #include <qwt_text.h> and #include <QPen>: types used as
     complete types but only forward-declared. Fixes 3 compile errors on macOS.

   src/_subclassQWT/mydialbox.cpp
   - Replace QColor::dark() / QColor::light() with darker() / lighter()
     throughout (6 occurrences). Deprecated in Qt 5.0, removed in Qt 6.

   .github/workflows/build.yml
   - Switch Windows SFML download from Invoke-WebRequest to curl.exe -L:
     handles SourceForge redirects correctly. Fixes Windows CI network failure.

   .github/workflows/codacy-analysis.yml
   - Remove Codacy security scan workflow entirely.

* Fix Windows and macOS CI build failures

Windows: QWT 6.2.0 ignores command-line QWT_INSTALL_PREFIX because
qwtconfig.pri overrides it with a hardcoded 'C:/Qwt-6.2.0' default.
Use 'qmake -after' so the assignment takes effect after .pro processing,
ensuring QWT installs to the expected ../qwt location where
PowerVelo.pro's include/lib paths point.

macOS: Add missing #include <QBrush> in zoneitem.cpp.
QBrush was used but not forward-declared via its own header,
causing 'no viable conversion from QColor to const QBrush'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix macOS QWT framework ODR error and Windows SFML retry

macOS: disable QwtFramework mode when building QWT to prevent Clang
from resolving the same header via two different canonical paths
(flat -I vs -F framework). This caused 'cannot convert WorkoutPlotZoomer*
to QwtPlot*' errors due to two distinct QwtPlot type identities.

Windows: add --retry 5 --retry-delay 5 --retry-connrefused to the SFML
curl download to handle transient network failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix macOS Clang type mismatch and Windows SFML download

macOS: replace markeritem.h / iconitem.h includes in workoutplotzoomer.h
with forward declarations. Including these headers pulled qwt_plot_item.h
via a double-quoted path from src/_subclassQWT/, which caused Clang on
macOS to see a different QwtPlot identity than the one WorkoutPlotZoomer
inherits from, resulting in 'cannot convert WorkoutPlotZoomer* to QwtPlot*'
errors. workoutplot.h (which works) never included those headers; the same
forward-declaration pattern fixes workoutplotzoomer.h.

Windows: change SFML download from www.sfml-dev.org (consistently
unreachable from GitHub Actions) to the official GitHub release mirror
(github.com/SFML/SFML/releases/download/2.6.1/).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix macOS QWT type mismatch: consistent angle-bracket QWT includes

Convert all double-quote QWT includes in workoutplotzoomer.cpp to
angle brackets (matching workoutplot.cpp which compiles fine), and
remove the duplicate #include util.h. Also add -fno-implicit-module-maps
to disable Clang's automatic Qt framework module imports, which can cause
the same type (e.g. QwtPlot, QFrame) to be seen as two different types
when included via different paths in the same translation unit on macOS.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix QWT include paths for Windows and macOS builds

Windows: replace qmake -after override with direct qwtconfig.pri patch
(like macOS sed approach) so QWT installs to the path PowerVelo.pro expects.

macOS: convert all double-quote QWT #include directives in workoutplotzoomer.h
and util.h to angle brackets so Clang sees a single consistent QwtPlot type
identity regardless of include search path (fixes WorkoutPlotZoomer* cannot
convert to QwtPlot* errors).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Windows: use QT_HOME env var and USERPROFILE\qwt for QWT; macOS: -fno-modules

Windows CI:
- Add QT_HOME=C:\Qt job-level env var so Qt install location is explicit and
  configurable for both CI and local Windows builds (jurplel/install-qt-action
  uses dir: QT_HOME, installing Qt to C:\Qt\Qt\5.15.2\msvc2019_64).
- Install QWT to %USERPROFILE%\qwt (stable, writable, works locally and in CI)
  instead of workspace-relative path; patch qwtconfig.pri line-by-line so all
  QWT_INSTALL_PREFIX assignments are redirected to that location.
- Set QMAKEFEATURES inside the PowerShell script using the same USERPROFILE path.

macOS CI:
- Replace -fno-implicit-module-maps with -fno-modules to fully disable Clang's
  Qt framework module system; this prevents the ODR type-identity mismatch where
  QwtPlot appears as two different types (WorkoutPlotZoomer* not convertible to
  QwtPlot*).
- Revert workoutplotzoomer.h QWT includes back to double-quote style (angle
  brackets introduced a QwtText incomplete-type regression).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* macOS: migrate to Qt6.7.3 ARM64, make VLC-Qt optional via GC_HAVE_VLCQT

- build.yml: use Qt 6.7.3 (clang_64 universal binary) on macos-latest ARM64

- src/main/main.cpp: guard myvlcplayer include with #ifdef GC_HAVE_VLCQT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Qt6: replace QLayout::setMargin() with setContentsMargins()

calls with layout->setContentsMargins(n, n, n, n).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix macOS ODR: disable QWT framework mode via sed in CI

QWT framework mode creates dual header include paths (flat + framework),
causing Clang ODR errors where WorkoutPlotZoomer* is not convertible to
QwtPlot*. Disabling framework mode forces plain shared lib install with
a single flat include path, eliminating the ambiguity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add agent.md: Qt build configuration rules per platform

Documents the deterministic Qt/QWT/VLC-Qt/SFML setup for Linux (Qt5),
Windows (Qt5) and macOS (Qt6 ARM64), including the QWT framework ODR
fix, GC_HAVE_VLCQT rules, Qt5->Qt6 breaking changes, and Windows path
conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove agent.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix macOS ODR: add -fno-modules; fix ANT.pri line continuations

-fno-modules disables Clang's implicit module system for Qt6 framework
headers. Without it, QwtPlot gets two type identities (one from QWT's
double-quote includes, another via Qt framework module imports), causing:
  cannot convert WorkoutPlotZoomer* to QwtPlot*

Also fix ANT.pri: lines 23-32 used '\' line-continuation with repeated
'INCLUDEPATH +=' which qmake parsed as literal 'INCLUDEPATH' and '+='
strings, generating garbage -IINCLUDEPATH and -I+= compiler flags.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix macOS QWT ODR: bypass qwt.prf, use flat QWT_INSTALL path

qwt.prf includes the installed qwtconfig.pri at project build time.
With Qt 6.7.3 (which uses frameworks), qwtconfig.pri detects
qt_framework in CONFIG and adds QwtFramework to QWT_CONFIG.
qwt.prf then sets INCLUDEPATH to qwt.framework/Headers instead of
the flat include/ directory. This creates two QwtPlot type identities
(flat headers from direct double-quote includes vs framework headers
from qwt_plot_item.h's chain), causing the Clang ODR error:
  cannot convert WorkoutPlotZoomer* to QwtPlot*

Fix: pass QWT_INSTALL=/usr/local/qwt-6.2.0 to qmake instead of using
QMAKEFEATURES. The macx block in PowerVelo.pro now manually adds the
flat INCLUDEPATH and -lqwt linkage, bypassing qwt.prf entirely.
Remove QMAKEFEATURES env var from the CI Create Makefile step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix macOS ODR: convert all QWT angle-bracket includes to double-quotes

Angle-bracket includes resolve via system search paths which can find
a different QWT installation (e.g. Homebrew qwt) before INCLUDEPATH.
This creates two QwtPlot/QwtText type identities in the same TU causing
the Clang ODR error: WorkoutPlotZoomer cannot convert to QwtPlot.

Convert all QWT angle-bracket includes to double-quotes in:
- src/main/util.h
- src/_subclassQWT/zoneitem.h
- src/_subclassQWT/workoutplotzoomer.cpp
- src/_subclassQWT/workoutplot.cpp
- src/_subclassQWT/workoutplotlist.cpp
- src/_subclassQWT/curvedata*.h

Also add brew uninstall qwt guard and diagnostic step to CI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix macOS: add missing qwt_text.h include to workoutplotzoomer.h

qwt_plot_textlabel.h and qwt_plot_marker.h only forward-declare
class QwtText, they do not include qwt_text.h. The workoutplotzoomer.h
header declares a value-type member 'QwtText valueQwtText' which
requires the full type definition. Without the direct include,
Clang reports 'field has incomplete type QwtText'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Qt6 mac build: QBluetoothUuid nested enums and QLowEnergyController API

- QLowEnergyController::error signal renamed to errorOccurred
- QLowEnergyService::DiscoveryRequired renamed to RemoteService
- QLowEnergyService::ServiceDiscovered renamed to RemoteServiceDiscovered

Guard all changes with QT_VERSION checks to preserve Qt5 compatibility.

Remove all ANT USB library source compilation on Windows. The USB stick
code has numerous MSVC/TCHAR incompatibilities and will be replaced by
the BTLE stack anyway.

* Fix Fit.pri: expand SOURCES wildcard for MSVC compatibility

qmake does not expand ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___$LASTEXITCODEPWD/*.cpp wildcards in SOURCES when generating
MSVC/nmake Makefiles — the literal glob is passed to cl.exe which fails.
List all .cpp files explicitly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Windows CI: use 8.3 short path for Windows Kit to fix LNK1181

The WINKIT_INSTALL path 'C:/Program Files (x86)/Windows Kits/...' has
spaces. qmake emits it unquoted in the linker response file, causing
the linker to treat 'Files' as 'Files.obj' (LNK1181).

Use GetShortPathName() to convert to an 8.3 path (no spaces) before
passing to qmake.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Windows: remove WINKIT_INSTALL — MSVC finds system libs via LIB env var

Gdi32 and User32 are resolved automatically by the MSVC linker using
the LIB environment variable set by the MSVC Developer Command Prompt.
The explicit -L path is unnecessary and caused LNK1181 because qmake
emitted the path (with spaces in 'Program Files (x86)') unquoted in
the linker response file.

commit.message:
[#10181] feat(spark-connector): Support TableWritePrivilege for Spark 3.5+ authorization (#10194)

### What changes were proposed in this pull request?

This PR adds support for Spark 3.5's TableWritePrivilege interface to
enforce MODIFY_TABLE privilege when performing write operations (INSERT,
UPDATE, DELETE). Changes:
- Add loadTable(Identifier, Set<TableWritePrivilege>) method to Spark
3.5+ catalogs (Hive, Iceberg, JDBC, Paimon)
- Add loadTableForWriting and loadGravitinoTableForWriting helper
methods in BaseCatalog
- Upgrade Spark 3.5 version from 3.5.1 to 3.5.3
- Add integration test to verify authorization behavior In Spark 3.5+,
when a user without MODIFY_TABLE privilege attempts to INSERT, a
ForbiddenException will be thrown. In Spark 3.3/3.4, the same operation
succeeds because those versions don't support TableWritePrivilege.

### Why are the changes needed?

Fix: #10181

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

No

### How was this patch tested?

Added tests.

* fix(website): remove three/wild-forest from sidebar until doc page exists

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ci): add PR test workflow, vitest coverage, and unit tests

Closes a degree of #82 by establishing an automated test baseline for every PR.
Applies reviewer feedback from #512 re: dev/ directory for WIP/RFC content.

## CI workflow (.github/workflows/test.yml)
- Triggers on push to master and all pull_request events
- lint job: runs yarn lint (no build required)
- test-node job: builds, runs vitest node project with v8 coverage, uploads to Coveralls
- test-headless job: installs Playwright Chromium, runs headless browser tests

Modeled after deck.gl's test.yml (see visgl/deck.gl vitest-migration-rfc) using
@vitest/browser + Playwright as first-class visgl testing utilities.

## Coverage (vitest.config.ts + package.json)
- Added test.coverage block: provider v8, reporters text + lcov (for Coveralls)
- Scoped to modules/*/src/**, excludes basemap-props* and template
- Added test-ci script: vitest run --project node --coverage
- Added @vitest/coverage-v8 devDependency

## New unit tests (all 308 pass, 60 test files)
- modules/editable-layers/test/immutable-feature-collection.node.spec.ts
  ImmutableFeatureCollection: add/delete/replace/removePosition, polygon ring
  wrapping, 3D elevation preservation, immutability guarantees (17 tests)
- modules/editable-layers/test/imports.node.spec.ts
  Public API surface smoke test: draw, alter, and measurement mode exports (6 tests)
- modules/layers/test/imports.node.spec.ts (2 tests)
- modules/widgets/test/imports.node.spec.ts (4 tests)
- modules/geo-layers/test/imports.node.spec.ts (3 tests)

## dev/ directory
Per ibgreen's feedback in #512: establishes dev/ as the canonical home for RFCs
and WIP content that should NOT be indexed by the docs site search.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove dev/ directory — RFCs move to GitHub Issues/Discussions

Per discussion: RFCs are better tracked as GitHub Issues or Discussions
where commenting and cross-linking are native. The docs tree stays focused
on true usability docs and basic rationale only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: flat dev/ for WIP modules and examples, published modules stay in modules/

Separates in-progress work from the published modules tree.
All npm-published 9.2.8 packages remain untouched in modules/.

Moved to dev/ (flat):
- modules/timeline-layers -> dev/timeline-layers (in progress, refs #517 #379)
  - examples/timeline-layers/horizon-graph-layer -> dev/timeline-layers/examples/
- examples/arrow-layers-wip -> dev/arrow-layers (renamed, drop -wip suffix)

Config:
- package.json + lerna.json: add dev/* to workspaces/packages
- tsconfig.json: add dev/ to include, add timeline-layers path alias
- vitest.config.ts: include dev/ in node/browser/headless globs and coverage
- keep modules/basemap-props/** excluded from node tests (uses global describe)

Rationale from #512: dev/ keeps WIP visible without polluting docs search
or the published modules tree. Design proposals live in GitHub Issues/Discussions.

commit.message:
test: add 148 CLI and UI integration tests

Add two new integration test files:

- crates/cli/tests/cli_hardening_tests.rs (74 tests)
  Covers subcommand parsing, help text, env var config, output formatting,
  error messages, exit codes, version display, shell completions, profile
  file handling, verbose flags, global flag ordering, and argument validation
  for telemetry/safety/diag/plugin subcommands.

- crates/ui/tests/ui_hardening_tests.rs (74 tests)
  Covers consent dialog state machine, combo instructions rendering,
  safety banner behavior, warning level styling/theming, error sanitisation,
  AppState/command data types, consent flow serialisation, and edge cases
  for extreme values and empty fields.

Both files follow project conventions:
- No unwrap()/expect() in tests
- Result-returning test functions
- Consistent use of type TestResult

commit.message:
test: add 138 diagnostic, telemetry stream, and pipeline tests

Add three new test files covering diagnostic system, telemetry streaming
layer, and FFB processing pipeline:

- diagnostic_hardening_tests.rs (46 tests): report generation, system
  health checks, RT diagnostics (latency/jitter metrics), error code
  catalog, support bundle generation, event serialization roundtrips,
  header/footer validation edge cases

- stream_hardening_tests.rs (52 tests): TelemetryBuffer creation and
  FIFO ordering, data rate enforcement via eviction, back-pressure
  handling with RingBuffer, concurrent multi-thread safety, stream
  lifecycle (start/stop/restart), MovingAverage/RateLimiter/RateCounter
  data format validation, error type display

- pipeline_hardening_tests.rs (40 tests): empty pipeline passthrough,
  response curve composition, sequential frame processing, sign
  preservation, curve override, error types, state snapshots and
  metrics, pipeline swap, config hash determinism, validator checks

All tests use Result-returning signatures (no unwrap/expect).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
